---
title: "Innovation"
description: "Business innovation in Canada: R&D, venture financing, patents, and high-technology exports"
---

Innovation turns research into new products, processes, and businesses — and into the productivity growth that raises living standards. Canada is strong on the *inputs*: it educates a highly skilled workforce, and its [researchers per capita](../science/index.qmd) rival the leaders. Yet on many measures of business innovation *output* — private-sector R&D, venture financing, high-value patents, and high-technology exports — it sits below most of its peers. That gap, and the economy's tilt toward resource and service industries rather than R&D-intensive manufacturing, is a long-standing question in Canadian economic-policy discussion.

The charts below place Canada against the 17-peer group across the innovation pipeline — from the money put in to the inventions and exports that come out. For the research base itself, see [Science](../science/index.qmd); for how much the economy invests overall, see [business investment](../economics/index.qmd#business-investment) and [productivity](../economics/index.qmd) on the Economy page.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import get_data_date, CANADA_COLOR, CATEGORICAL_COLORS, CATEGORICAL_OTHER
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar, category_bar, lines_over_time


def _peer_line(path, value_col, yaxis_title, source, **kw):
    """Standard peer line for an innovation indicator (source note below slider)."""
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", value_col, title="", yaxis_title=yaxis_title,
        show_average=True, source_note=f"{source} | Data as of: {get_data_date(path)}", **kw)
    return fig


_CFG = {"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
```

## Business R&D (BERD, % of GDP)

The clearest gauge of business-led innovation is how much the private sector itself spends on research and development. Canadian business R&D has run well below the peer average for decades — a core fact behind the country's productivity gap.

```{python}
#| echo: false
berd_path = "data/science/oecd_berd.csv"
if os.path.exists(berd_path):
    fig = _peer_line(berd_path, "berd_pct_gdp", "Business R&D (% of GDP)",
                     "Source: OECD MSTI", topical=["KOR"], initial_start=2000)
    fig.update_layout(meta=dict(autoscaleY=True))
    fig.show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Business R&D data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(berd_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(berd_path)
    ranked_bar(df, "berd_pct_gdp", "Business R&D (% of GDP)",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(berd_path)}",
        min_countries=15, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f}%<extra></extra>").show(config=_CFG)
```

## Venture capital

Young, research-intensive firms need risk capital to grow. Venture-capital investment, as a share of GDP, shows how much of that financing an economy mobilizes. Canada has built a larger venture market than most peers, but it remains well below the United States, the natural benchmark among large economies. (A few small economies, notably Israel, mobilize even more relative to GDP, but their specialised startup ecosystems make them outliers rather than like-for-like comparisons.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
vc_path = "data/science/oecd_venture_capital.csv"
if os.path.exists(vc_path):
    _peer_line(vc_path, "vc_pct_gdp", "Venture capital (% of GDP)",
               "Source: OECD, Venture Capital Investments").show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Venture-capital data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(vc_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(vc_path)
    ranked_bar(df, "vc_pct_gdp", "Venture capital (% of GDP)",
        f"Source: OECD, Venture Capital Investments | Data as of: {get_data_date(vc_path)}",
        min_countries=12, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f}%<extra></extra>").show(config=_CFG)
```

## High-value patents

Patent counts reward quantity, and most patents are low-value domestic filings. **Triadic patent families** — inventions patented at the European, US, and Japanese offices together — screen those out, so they track genuinely commercial, internationally protected inventions. Per million people, Canada produces far fewer than the research powerhouses (Switzerland, Japan, Korea), despite a comparable research workforce — a sign that more of Canada's research base stays upstream rather than becoming protected, commercialized invention.

```{python}
#| echo: false
pat_path = "data/science/oecd_triadic_patents.csv"
if os.path.exists(pat_path):
    _peer_line(pat_path, "patents_per_million", "Triadic patents per million people",
               "Source: OECD MSTI", topical=["CHE", "JPN"]).show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Patent data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(pat_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(pat_path)
    ranked_bar(df, "patents_per_million", "Triadic patents per million people",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(pat_path)}",
        min_countries=15, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>").show(config=_CFG)
```

## High-technology exports

Innovation shows up in what a country sells. High-technology exports — aerospace, pharmaceuticals, electronics, scientific instruments — as a share of manufactured exports, measure how research-intensive a country's traded goods are. Canada's share sits below most peers and well under the leaders (Israel, Korea, Switzerland).

```{python}
#| echo: false
hte_path = "data/science/worldbank_hightech_exports.csv"
if os.path.exists(hte_path):
    _peer_line(hte_path, "hightech_exports_pct", "High-tech exports (% of manufactured exports)",
               "Source: World Bank, World Development Indicators", topical=["KOR"]).show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("High-tech exports data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(hte_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hte_path)
    ranked_bar(df, "hightech_exports_pct", "High-tech exports (% of manufactured exports)",
        f"Source: World Bank, World Development Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(hte_path)}",
        min_countries=15, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>").show(config=_CFG)
```

## How governments back business R&D

Most governments help fund business R&D in two ways: **direct** funding (grants and contracts) and **tax incentives** (credits that lower the cost of research, like Canada's Scientific Research and Experimental Development — SR&ED — program). Canada is distinctive for leaning on tax credits far more than direct grants — a deliberate policy choice that several reviews have debated. This shows the latest-year split for each peer, Canada outlined.

```{python}
#| echo: false
rs_path = "data/science/oecd_rd_support.csv"
if os.path.exists(rs_path):
    rs = pd.read_csv(rs_path)
    yr = int(rs[rs["country_code"] == "CAN"]["year"].max())
    cur = rs[rs["year"] == yr].sort_values("total")        # ascending → largest on top
    border = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else "rgba(0,0,0,0)" for c in cur["country_code"]]
    bw = [2.2 if c == "CAN" else 0 for c in cur["country_code"]]
    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=cur["country_name"], x=cur["direct"], name="Direct funding",
        orientation="h", marker=dict(color=CATEGORICAL_COLORS[0], line=dict(color=border, width=bw)),
        hovertemplate="%{y} — direct: %{x:.2f}% of GDP<extra></extra>"))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(y=cur["country_name"], x=cur["tax"], name="Tax incentives",
        orientation="h", marker=dict(color=CATEGORICAL_COLORS[1], line=dict(color=border, width=bw)),
        hovertemplate="%{y} — tax: %{x:.2f}% of GDP<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", plot_bgcolor="white", height=560,
        xaxis=dict(title="Government support for business R&D (% of GDP)", ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(title=""), legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, x=0),
        margin=dict(l=10, r=20, t=40, b=80))
    fig.add_annotation(text=f"Source: OECD R&D Tax Incentive indicators | {yr}. Canada outlined; a country reporting only one type is shown as ~zero of the other.",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.13, xanchor="left", showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("R&D support data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## The economy behind the gap

Part of the explanation is structural. Canada's economy leans toward resource extraction, finance, and real estate more than the R&D-intensive manufacturing that drives most private research in peer economies — and a large share of the innovation Canada does produce is owned or performed from outside the country.

### Canada's economy, by industry

Real estate is the largest sector, followed by manufacturing, health and social services, finance, and professional services. Mining, oil and gas (highlighted alongside finance) is a significant share, concentrated in a few provinces. Together, finance, real estate and resource industries make up a substantial part of the economy — more than manufacturing and the knowledge-intensive professional and information services that perform most private R&D.

```{python}
#| echo: false
is_path = "data/science/statcan_industry_structure.csv"
if os.path.exists(is_path):
    isd = pd.read_csv(is_path)
    isd["lbl"] = [f"{v:.1f}%" for v in isd["share"]]
    per = isd["period"].iloc[0]
    category_bar(isd, "sector", "share", xaxis_title="Share of GDP (%)",
        ticksuffix="%", value_fmt=".1f", height=600, color=CATEGORICAL_OTHER,
        highlight={"Mining, oil & gas", "Finance & insurance"}, highlight_color=CATEGORICAL_COLORS[1],
        text_col="lbl", text_fmt="{}",
        hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}% of GDP<extra></extra>",
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 36-10-0434-01 (GDP by industry, chained 2017 $) | {per}").show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Industry-structure data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### Who performs Canada's business R&D

More than a third of the research and development performed by businesses in Canada is carried out by **foreign-controlled firms** — a share that has held around 37–39% for a decade. Foreign investment brings real R&D activity to Canada, but it also means a large part of the country's business research is performed by firms headquartered elsewhere.

```{python}
#| echo: false
fc_path = "data/science/statcan_foreign_rd_control.csv"
if os.path.exists(fc_path):
    fc = pd.read_csv(fc_path)
    fc["series"] = "Foreign-controlled firms"
    lines_over_time(fc, "year", "foreign_share", "series",
        yaxis_title="% of business in-house R&D", group_order=["Foreign-controlled firms"],
        yticksuffix="%", hover_decimals=1,
        source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 27-10-0333-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(fc_path)}").show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Foreign-control data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### Where invention value goes

Inventions made in one country are often owned by companies in another. About a quarter of the patents invented in Canada are owned by **US entities — the highest share of any peer country** — a concrete measure of how closely Canadian invention is tied to firms south of the border.

```{python}
#| echo: false
po_path = "data/science/oecd_patents_us_owned.csv"
if os.path.exists(po_path):
    _peer_line(po_path, "pct_us_owned", "Patents owned by US entities (%)",
               "Source: OECD, Foreign ownership of domestic inventions").show(config=_CFG)
else:
    print("Patent-ownership data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(po_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(po_path)
    ranked_bar(df, "pct_us_owned", "Domestic inventions owned by US entities (%)",
        f"Source: OECD, Foreign ownership of domestic inventions | Data as of: {get_data_date(po_path)}",
        min_countries=12, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>").show(config=_CFG)
```

## Download data

- [Business R&D, BERD (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_berd.csv) — OECD MSTI
- [Venture capital investment (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_venture_capital.csv) — OECD, Venture Capital Investments
- [Triadic patent families per million (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_triadic_patents.csv) — OECD MSTI
- [High-technology exports (CSV)](../data/science/worldbank_hightech_exports.csv) — World Bank, World Development Indicators
- [Government support for business R&D, direct vs tax (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_rd_support.csv) — OECD R&D Tax Incentive indicators
- [Canada's GDP by industry (CSV)](../data/science/statcan_industry_structure.csv) — Statistics Canada 36-10-0434-01
- [Foreign-controlled share of business R&D (CSV)](../data/science/statcan_foreign_rd_control.csv) — Statistics Canada 27-10-0333-01
- [Domestic inventions owned by US entities (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_patents_us_owned.csv) — OECD, Foreign ownership of domestic inventions
